methodsAttempted.query: site:aalto.fi Aalto University generative AI studies policy artificial intelligence students assessment
methodsAttempted.notes: Found official Aalto teaching, student, tool, and research guidance pages.
One work guidance page redirected to login and was recorded as inaccessible.
Finnish and Swedish variants were found, but the English official pages contained directly usable original-language evidence.

artifacts.summary: Official Aalto pages provided public evidence for teaching and learning, student use, productized AI services, Aalto AI Assistant privacy boundaries, and responsible AI use in research. One staff work guidance page required login and was not used.

Tips for using artificial intelligence for students

artifacts.claimText: Aalto University guidance says AI-based technologies are allowed as support for teaching and learning unless the course teacher instructs otherwise, and the teacher may set course- or task-specific restrictions when learning objectives require them.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for the use of artificial intelligence in teaching and learning at Aalto University
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for the use of artificial intelligence in teaching and learning at Aalto University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The use of AI-based technologies is allowed as a support for teaching and learning unless instructed otherwise by the teacher of the course. The teacher of the course may decide on restrictions on the use of technology on a course- or task-specific basis if achieving the learning objectives of the course requires it.

artifacts.claimText: Aalto University guidance says teachers who restrict AI use must give clear limitations connected to assessment criteria, and teachers may only require student work to be submitted through systems approved by Aalto University.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for the use of artificial intelligence in teaching and learning at Aalto University
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for the use of artificial intelligence in teaching and learning at Aalto University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If the teacher decides to restrict the use of AI on a course- or task-specific basis, they must provide clear instructions on the limitations in connection with the assessment criteria. The teacher may only submit student work or require students themselves to submit student work in systems approved by Aalto University.

artifacts.claimText: Aalto University guidance says students remain responsible for submitted work, and using AI contrary to a teacher's instructions in a learning task is considered cheating and handled under current procedures.

citation.citationTitle: Guidance for the use of artificial intelligence in teaching and learning at Aalto University

artifacts.sourceTitle: Guidance for the use of artificial intelligence in teaching and learning at Aalto University
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: The student is always responsible for the content of their submitted work. Utilising AI in a learning task contrary to the teacher instructions will be considered cheating and will be handled in accordance with the current procedures.

artifacts.claimText: Aalto student guidance says students should contact the course teacher if AI use is unclear, mark AI-generated content in their text, and not present AI-generated content as their own.

citation.citationTitle: Tips for using artificial intelligence for students
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Tips for using artificial intelligence for students
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: If you are unsure whether you can use a specific system or application in completing the task, always contact the course teacher. Mark the content generated by artificial intelligence in your text and include references to the name of the AI model or service, the date, and when the content was generated. Do not present content generated by artificial intelligence as your own.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Aalto University AI Services describes Aalto AI Assistant as an official productized AI service available to all Aaltonians, with GPT models that can be applied to public, internal, and confidential information.

citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Here are Aalto University official and productized AI services. Aalto AI Assistant is an AI tool that utilises large language models. The GPT models are secure and can be applied to public and internal information as well as confidential data. Aalto AI Assistant is available to all Aaltonians.

artifacts.claimText: Aalto University research guidance says AI cannot be given authorship, AI use in the research process should be transparently described for reproducibility, and personal data should not be fed into an online AI tool.

citation.citationTitle: Responsible use of Artificial Intelligence in the research process
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Responsible use of Artificial Intelligence in the research process
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: AI cannot be given authorship, as it is considered as a tool, and authorship always involves responsibility that AI cannot cover. The use of AI should be transparent by openly describing how AI is used in the research process so that others can reproduce your results. GDPR is to be followed, meaning that research data containing personal data should not be fed into an online AI tool.
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.title: Aalto University staged AI policy evidence summary
artifacts.summary: Staged candidate claims summarize official Aalto public guidance on AI in teaching and learning, student use, Aalto AI Assistant privacy boundaries, productized AI services, and responsible AI use in research. A login-gated staff work guidance page was recorded but not used as evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university own official page.
Aalto staff work guidance requiring login was not accessed or used.
